title: lycium intricatum subsp. intricatum
intro: Lycium intricatum subsp. intricatum, also known as the intricate matthiola or desert boxthorn, is a shrub-like plant species native to Australia, characterized by its thorny stems and small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. The health benefits of Lycium intricatum subsp. intricatum include its potential to support eye health, reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules, and are often used to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
